description_long: This is a short, two-rail fence constructed with two vertical posts and two horizontal rails. The posts are a natural wood color, while the rails are painted white, creating a simple yet visually distinct contrast. The rails are mounted parallel to each other, with a slight offset, and are rectangular in cross-section. The top of the fence is flat, with the posts extending slightly above the upper rail. There are no decorative elements or post caps, giving the fence a utilitarian and modern appearance. This type of fence is commonly used as a barrier or demarcation in urban parks, sidewalks, or residential areas.
description: A simple, two-rail fence with wood-colored posts and white horizontal rails, featuring a flat top and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing both vertical posts and the two white horizontal rails clearly, with the offset between the rails visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the fence, with the rails and posts aligned in depth.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the posts and rails from the opposite side, with the same flat top and parallel rails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the rails and posts.
